name: Terri Lyne Carrington
abstract: Terri Lyne Carrington (born August 4, 1965) is an American jazz drummer, composer, producer, and educator. She has played with Dizzy Gillespie, Stan Getz, Clark Terry, Herbie Hancock, Wayne Shorter, Joe Sample, Al Jarreau, Yellowjackets, and many others.

interviews.title: Terri Lyne Carrington interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 9, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Barron
about_quotes.evidence: Terri Lyne Carrington was playing drums, and Alex Blake playing electric bass and acoustic bass. So that must be the one they are talking about, 1990.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Barron — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Toots Thielemans
about_quotes.evidence: I listen to… for instance, this lady, Terri Lyne Carrington. Isn’t she [mean?] [inaudible]. I think she’s mean, you know.
about_quotes.doc_title: Toots Thielemans — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jane Bunnett
about_quotes.evidence: I look to somebody like Terri Lyne Carrington to answer those questions and I really admire her
